name: create-plan
description: Create comprehensive implementation plan in .plans directory based on analysis or report. Use when user asks to create a plan, plan implementation, design a solution, or structure work for a feature/refactor/fix.
capabilities: Creates structured implementation plans in .plans directory
Provides templates for features, refactors, and bug fixes
Maps every requirement to implementation steps
author: Cygnusfear
securitySummary: The create-plan skill is purely documentation-based with no executable code. All 124 static findings are false positives triggered by markdown documentation patterns. The skill contains only planning templates and guidelines for software development. No security risks exist.
valueStatement: Stop wasting time figuring out how to structure complex development work. This skill creates detailed implementation plans that guide you step-by-step through feature development, refactoring, or bug fixes.
userTitle: Generate Implementation Plans for Development Tasks
limitations: Requires existing codebase analysis for accurate plans
Cannot execute code or modify files directly
Plans must be manually implemented by developers
Depends on quality of input requirements

useCases.title: Plan Complex Refactoring Projects
useCases.description: Break down large-scale refactoring into manageable steps with clear dependencies and validation checkpoints.

useCases.title: Create Feature Implementation Roadmaps
useCases.description: Generate comprehensive plans for new features that account for all requirements and edge cases.

useCases.title: Structure Bug Fix Work
useCases.description: Get step-by-step guidance for fixing bugs with proper testing and validation steps.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Feature Plan
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding a new user authentication system
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an implementation plan for adding JWT-based authentication to our Node.js API. Users should be able to register, login, and access protected routes.

promptTemplates.title: Refactoring Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Plan the refactoring of our monolithic Express app into a modular architecture with separate controllers, services, and data layers.

promptTemplates.title: Bug Fix Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a plan to fix the memory leak in our WebSocket connection handler that's causing server crashes every 6 hours.

promptTemplates.title: Complex Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an implementation plan for integrating our payment system with Stripe, including webhook handling, error recovery, and database transactions.

bestPractices: Always extract and verify all requirements before creating the plan
Use the appropriate template (feature/refactor/fix) for your task type
Review existing CLAUDE.md files to ensure compliance with project standards
antiPatterns: Creating plans without auditing the existing codebase first
Using migration strategies or gradual rollouts (explicitly forbidden)
Including temporary code or fallback mechanisms in plans

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with all project types?
faq.answer: Yes, it works with any codebase but requires existing project structure analysis for best results.

faq.question: What are the size limits for implementation plans?
faq.answer: Plans can handle projects of any size, but very large projects should be broken into smaller phases.

faq.question: Can I integrate these plans with my project management tools?
faq.answer: Plans are saved as markdown files in .plans directory, easily convertible to other formats.

faq.question: How does the skill ensure data safety?
faq.answer: The skill only reads and creates documentation files, never modifying your actual code.

faq.question: What if the plan does not match my requirements?
faq.answer: The skill validates against original requirements - simply provide more specific details in your request.

faq.question: How does this compare to manual planning?
faq.answer: It provides structured, comprehensive plans following best practices while saving significant time.
